description: Silybum marianum (milk thistle) is often chosen for liver-focused supplement routines that prioritize standardized silymarin labeling.
dosage: Common adult use is 140 mg standardized extract (about 70-80% silymarin) two to three times daily.
duration: Frequently used in medium-length daily routines with periodic review of medication interactions.
preparation: Capsules and softgels are the main formats for consistent labeled intake.

therapeuticUses: Single-herb protocols that avoid complex detox blends
categoryUseContext: Compared for liver-support category use, standardized extract purchasing, and conservative single-herb protocols.
evidenceLevel: Moderate evidence in selected liver-related contexts; formulation quality and standardization drive comparability.
